discovery_instructions: Identify what the main item/entity is for this feature (e.g., employee record, document, invitation)
Look for list/table views - the rows represent the primary entity
Check page headers and breadcrumbs for entity naming
Note the singular and plural forms used in the UI

find_trigger: Look for buttons with: + Add, + Create, + New, plus icon
Check top-right corner of the page
Look in empty state messages for create prompts
create.validation: New item appears in list with unique identifier

find_trigger: Click on any row in a list/table
Look for 'View' or eye icon buttons
Check if rows are clickable (cursor changes)
read.validation: Detail panel, modal, or page opens showing full information

find_trigger: Look for Edit button, pencil icon
Check for inline editing (click on field to edit)
Look for status change dropdowns or buttons
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for edit option
update.validation: Changes persist after save, reflected in list view

find_trigger: Look for Delete button, trash icon
Check action menus (three-dot menu) for delete option
Look for bulk delete with checkboxes
Some features use 'Archive' or 'Cancel' instead of delete
delete.validation: Item removed from list or status changed to deleted/archived

instructions: Navigate to the feature's main section using the navigation_map
Take a full page snapshot to understand the layout
Find ALL clickable elements: buttons, links, tabs, dropdowns, icons
Map the navigation structure: tabs, sidebar items, breadcrumbs
Click each tab/sub-section and document what appears
Locate CRUD controls for the primary entity
Check for action menus (three-dot menus, right-click menus)
Document any modals, dialogs, slide-out panels
Identify form fields and input types when forms appear
Look for filters, search, sort, and export options
Check for bulk action controls (checkboxes, select all)
Note any settings or configuration options
discovery_targets: List/table of items with row actions

content_clusters.consolidation_notes: Merged articles on unpaid leave setup, leave salary policy configuration, and accrual pausing with PRDs on tenure-based configuration and dynamic pay rates. All sources address HR admin configuration workflows for creating and managing leave policies with various conditions and restrictions.

Consolidated articles on leave salary request management and leave salary transactions with PRDs on leave salary opportunity and detailed PRD. All sources cover Finance Manager and HR Admin workflows for processing advance leave salary payments, calculating prorated amounts, and integrating with payroll.

Merged articles on editing leave balance and unpaid leave deductions with PRD on unpaid leave deductions automation. All sources address HR Admin workflows for manually adjusting leave balances and managing automatic payroll deductions for unpaid leave.

Three articles cover leave calendar viewing from admin and employee perspectives, plus configuration to enable employee visibility. PRD 3375169622 enriches with team-level sharing levels (Reporting Manager, Department, Office, All Employees) and color-coding logic (orange=pending, green=approved).

Articles cover employee leave request submission, editing at different approval stages, and admin editing capabilities. PRDs add approval flow configuration (standard and advanced) with customizable steps, role-based approvers, and sequential/parallel approval options.

Articles cover leave encashment calculation, unpaid leave setup with pay deductions, and public holiday deduction automation. PRD adds automated unpaid leave deduction integration with payroll module. Consolidated into single task for HR Admins configuring leave deductions and encashment.
Single article on restricting leave during probation. Straightforward configuration task for HR Admins.
Grouped tickets addressing missing error handling and database constraints when leave requests span multiple cycles or when updating cycle settings.
Grouped tickets where automated notifications are missing for leave balance expiration, policy changes, and holiday impacts on existing requests.
Grouped tickets addressing poor calendar UX for approvals, lack of filtering by direct reports, and broken notification navigation.
Grouped tickets where bulk update capabilities are missing for leave balances, request statuses, and leave type configurations.
Grouped tickets addressing lack of flexible editing capabilities for leave requests and missing salary configuration reminders for unpaid leave.
Single ticket addressing critical control gap where offboarding employees can exploit negative balance to request leave.
Grouped tickets addressing tooltip persistence and missing auto-refresh in attendance module after leave request creation.

Three tickets addressing inability to work with past dates and historical leave cycles. AV-10078 affects reporting visibility, AV-244 affects leave request submission for non-existent cycles, AV-1102 affects retrospective leave entry. All stem from rigid date validation logic.
Single ticket addressing inability to change leave type classification after initial setup. Affects leave policy flexibility.
Single ticket addressing inability to reconcile work hours after half-day leave. Affects employee day-to-day usage and attendance tracking.

product_context.title: Opp Assessment: Leaves Configuration Phase 1
product_context.why_built: Organizations need to comply with UAE and KSA labor laws that require different leave allowances based on employee tenure. Manual policy switching and leave adjustments are time-consuming and error-prone. Dynamic tenure-based allowance configuration eliminates this burden by automatically adjusting leave entitlements as employees reach tenure milestones.
key_design_decisions: Pre-built labor law compliant leave policy templates (UAE/KSA) reduce setup time and ensure compliance
Dynamic allowance tiers based on tenure thresholds with automatic calculation
System auto-adjusts allowance when employee crosses tenure threshold (no manual intervention needed)
HR can unassign employees from policy if needed
Dynamic allowance tiers based on tenure thresholds eliminate need for multiple policies per tenure tier
System automatically calculates leave allowance based on employee tenure
Cannot currently unassign employees from leave policy (product limitation being fixed)
Dynamic allowance tiers based on tenure thresholds automatically adjust leave entitlements without requiring policy reassignment
Employees view policy details and entitlements in Leaves module for transparency

product_context.title: Opp Assessment: Leaves Configuration Phase 2
product_context.why_built: Improve integration between Leaves and Payroll modules by automating partial/dynamic pay rate calculations for leave policies. Users need to comply with UAE & KSA labor laws requiring different pay rates based on total leave days taken (sick leave, maternity leave), eliminate manual payroll deductions for partial pay periods, and manage leave policies without creating multiple policies for different pay rate scenarios.
key_design_decisions: Dynamic pay rate configuration allows conditional pay rates based on total leave days taken for compliance (sick leave, maternity leave)
Automatic payroll deduction without manual adjustment
Enhanced leave balance breakdown on mobile app
Future capability to pause accrual during unpaid leave and exclude unpaid leave from tenure calculations
Dynamic pay rate configuration for specific leave policies
Automatic pay rate calculation based on days taken without manual adjustment
Payroll deduction automation eliminates manual adjustments for partial pay leaves
Enhanced leave balance breakdown (mobile) provides employees visibility into how their leave allowance is calculated
Future phases will enable updating leave allowance in existing policies, pausing accrual during unpaid leave, and excluding unpaid leave from job tenure calculations

product_context.title: Leave Salary Product Opportunity Assessment
product_context.why_built: Enable companies to process advance leave salary payments within Bayzat payroll, eliminating manual Excel-based tracking. In UAE and KSA, employees (especially blue-collar workers traveling home) receive salary for paid leave before the leave starts to cover travel expenses. Currently payroll managers spend considerable time managing this separately, causing some employers to abandon the practice due to complexity.
key_design_decisions: Single entry point through leave request for leave salary process
Configurable calendar/working days earned per month
Selectable allowances included in leave salary calculation
Option for full 30-day or specified day payment rules
Leave salary can be processed as part of monthly payroll OR as out-of-payroll transaction
Payroll table auto-adjusts for employees on leave
WPS/non-WPS SIF file generation for wage protection reporting
Supporting documents can be attached (air tickets, declarations)
System calculates leave salary based on configuration
WPS/non-WPS SIF files generated automatically
Goal: reduce leave salary processing time by 80% with maximum 2 steps

product_context.title: Leave Salary PRD
product_context.why_built: In UAE and KSA, employees (especially blue-collar workers traveling home) need advance vacation salaries before their leave starts to cover travel expenses. Companies were managing this separately in Excel, causing friction and potentially losing customers who needed integrated leave salary processing. Automating leave salary reduces processing time by 80% and increases platform stickiness.
key_design_decisions: Leave salary and encashment toggles are independent - can enable one without the other
Multiple paid vacation types can be combined into one leave salary setting
Probation restriction can block leave salary for employees on probation
Salary proration calculates pay for days worked in the month (before/after leave period)
Leave salary and encashment only available for Paid leave types - unpaid leave cannot enable these features
Unpaid leave types cannot enable leave salary/encashment - system enforces with tooltip
Automatic leave salary request creation when toggle enabled
Automatic leave salary toggle enables system to auto-create leave salary requests when employee applies for leave

product_context.title: PRD: Unpaid leave deductions
product_context.why_built: Automate unpaid leave deductions by integrating Time Off module with Payroll module. Eliminate manual data transfer between modules when employees take unpaid leave, automatically calculating and applying salary deductions based on approved unpaid leave requests.
key_design_decisions: Automatic deduction creation upon full leave approval
Multi-month leave creates separate deductions per calendar month
Deduction cannot be edited once created—must adjust leave request to recalculate (maintains data consistency)
Cannot create deductions for closed payroll months (handled via email notification)
Daily pay formula: Salary Amount / Total days (calendar or working based on policy)
Upon full approval of unpaid leave request, system automatically creates payroll deduction
Deduction appears in payroll adjustments with calculated amount
Future month deductions scheduled but email notification delayed until month opens
Closed payroll months cannot receive new deductions - handled via email notification
Salary = Basic + Allowances (if selected)

product_context.title: Opportunity Assessment: Leave Calendar customizations
product_context.why_built: Enable transparent leave visibility between team members so employees can make better leave planning decisions that minimize impact on work and business operations. Currently employees can only see their own leaves and public holidays, leading to back-and-forth communication to check availability. With 56 votes on Aha!, this addresses a key need for coordinated time-off planning within teams.
key_design_decisions: Feature disabled by default - Super Admin must explicitly enable
Configurable sharing levels: Same Reporting Manager, Same Department, Same Office, All Employees
Uses existing profile data (Department, Reports To, Office) - no custom team management in Phase 1
Pending leaves shown in orange, approved in green on calendar
When viewing others' leave details, hide Attachment and Message fields for privacy
Phase 1 limited to website only (no mobile developer available)

product_context.title: Opp Assessment: Leaves Approval Flow (Standard)
product_context.why_built: Organizations needed customizable leave approval workflows to replace hacky workarounds (dummy accounts, manual off-platform tracking). Standard approval flow provides company-wide configuration with customizable steps, sequential approval enforcement, and multiple approvers for redundancy.
key_design_decisions: Standard flow applies single approval configuration company-wide (unlike Advanced which varies by department/office/policy)
Customizable approval steps with role-based approvers
Sequential approval enforces critical approver first; parallel allows any approver
Multiple approvers per step provide redundancy and continuity when someone is unavailable
Ultimate approver role can bypass other approvers for urgent situations
Eliminates workarounds like dummy accounts and manual off-platform tracking
Depends on existing leave approval flow system and role management system

product_context.title: Opp Assessment: Leaves Approval Flow (Advanced)
product_context.why_built: Large organizations with complex business structures needed different leave approval workflows based on leave policy type, department, or office. Standard approval flow was too rigid. Advanced flow enables custom roles (Sales Director, CEO, Department Manager, Office Manager) as approvers instead of only standard Line Manager hierarchy.
key_design_decisions: Different approval flows can vary by leave policy, department, or office
Custom roles (Sales Director, CEO, Department Manager, Office Manager) as approvers instead of only standard Line Manager hierarchy
Specific employees or custom roles can be assigned as approvers
Approval sequence can be configured with critical approvers first
Workaround/delegation feature handles approver unavailability to prevent bottlenecks
Advanced flow allows different workflows per leave policy, department, or office (unlike Standard which is company-wide)
Custom roles (Sales Director, CEO, Department Manager, Office Manager) work similar to Line Manager (auto-assigned based on structure)
Targets large organizations (250+) and small organizations (20-250)

product_context.title: Opportunity Assessment: Unpaid Leave requests
product_context.why_built: Enable employers to track unpaid leave requests and understand salary deductions associated with unpaid leave for each employee per month. Provide visibility into unpaid leave impact on payroll calculations.
key_design_decisions: Employee submits unpaid leave request through Time Off module
Manager/HR approves or rejects request
System tracks approved unpaid leave days
Payroll automatically calculates salary deduction
HR views overview of unpaid leave deductions per employee per month
Reports generated on unpaid leave across organization

product_context.title: Online doctor - Sick Leave Certificates - Opportunity assessment
product_context.why_built: Enable employees to obtain Sick Leave Certificates (SLCs) through online doctor consultations instead of 2-3 hour hospital visits. For HR admins, provide centralized SLC tracking and storage to eliminate the mess of collecting certificates from multiple sources (email, messengers, physical) in different formats. Customers receive 250-1000 SLCs monthly - integrating this with Time-Off module increases platform usage and stickiness.
key_design_decisions: Integration with Time-Off module for sick leave requests
Online doctor / HAH teleconsultation platform integration
Checkout.com payment integration (AED 50 per consultation)
DHA official SLC issuance through HAH
Centralized SLC tracking and storage for admins
Automatic SLC sharing with employer via platform

detected_integrations.description: Leave policies integrate with payroll for automated deductions (unpaid leave, partial pay), leave encashment calculations, and leave salary advance payments. Payroll module must be enabled for these features. Leave salary transactions are processed as part of monthly payroll or as separate out-of-payroll transactions. WPS/SIF files include leave salary transactions.

Leave policies define the rules for leave requests. Leave requests trigger approval workflows, automatic deductions, and leave salary calculations. Leave request status updates affect leave balance tracking and payroll processing.

Leave policies and approval workflows depend on employee profile data: probation dates (for probation restrictions), tenure/start date (for tenure-based allowances), salary information (for deduction calculations), department/office (for advanced approval flows), and reporting line (for line manager approvals).
Leave salary transactions are included in WPS SIF file generation for wage protection reporting. System generates both WPS and non-WPS SIF files for leave salary processing.
Payroll calendar month status (open/closed) determines whether unpaid leave deductions can be created. Closed payroll months require manual email notification to Payroll Manager.

Leave requests go through hierarchical approval workflow. Full approval triggers automatic payroll deduction creation for unpaid leave. Approval status determines when leave salary requests are created.
Unpaid leave deductions and leave salary adjustments appear in payroll adjustments page for review and tracking.
System sends notifications to payroll processor when unpaid leave deductions are created, especially for future month deductions and closed payroll month scenarios.
Employee structures enable automatic employee assignment in leave policies. Without a structure, the Manage employee assignment button is disabled.
Related feature for paying out unused leave. Leave salary and encashment toggles are independent but both depend on leave policy configuration.
Displays unpaid leave deductions created from approved leave requests. Payroll processor manages deductions from this interface.
Centralized location for leave calendar visibility settings, leave policy configuration, leave salary policies, and approval flow setup.
Employees can submit and edit leave requests, view leave calendar, and access leave balance information on mobile platform.
External integration for sick leave certificate issuance through teleconsultation (future feature).
Payment processing for online doctor consultations (AED 50 per session) for sick leave certificates (future feature).
Official issuer of sick leave certificates through HAH platform integration (future feature).
Approval flow configuration depends on role management for assigning Line Manager permissions and custom roles (Sales Director, CEO, Department Manager, Office Manager) as approvers.
Public holiday calendar integrates with leave policies to automatically exclude public holidays from leave calculations. Changes to public holidays trigger recalculation of affected leave requests.

Leave request notifications are sent to managers via Bayzat App and email. Approval flow notifications alert approvers when action is required. Public holiday changes trigger optional email notifications to affected employees.
Leave salary transactions are recorded in accounting for accrual and expense tracking. Leave encashment affects end-of-service liability calculations.
Leave salary transactions appear on employee salary slips. Employees view leave balance, accrued days, and expected leave salary payments in self-service portal.
Leave requests can include supporting documents (air tickets, declarations for leave salary). Documents are stored and linked to leave requests.
Leave data is used for reporting: unpaid leave deductions per employee per month, leave salary overview, leave encashment calculations, and leave analytics across organization.
Leave requests update attendance records. Attendance table does not auto-refresh after leave request creation, requiring manual page reload.
Public holiday updates can impact existing leave requests. System lacks notification mechanism to alert HR and managers when holiday changes affect pending leave requests.
Offboarding employees should have restricted leave request capabilities. Current system lacks controls to prevent leave requests during exit workflows.
Leave request approval notifications do not navigate to the relevant leave request screen, breaking the approval workflow UX.
Leave balance reporting requires access to historical leave cycle data and proper filtering mechanisms (AV-10078).

check_instructions: Navigate to Automations in the left sidebar
Click + Create workflow (or similar create button)
In trigger selection, search for this feature's name
Check if this feature appears as a trigger source
Document any trigger events found (e.g., 'item created', 'status changed')
Check Actions section for actions related to this feature
Look for 'Approval' or 'Pending Approval' status in the feature
Check if items have approval workflow indicators
Look for 'Submit for Approval' buttons or actions
Find approval settings in feature configuration
Document the approval chain (who approves what)
Test approval/rejection flow if test data available
Check for approval history or audit trail

what_to_do.task: Create and Configure Leave Policies with Pay Rate Conditions
steps: Navigate to Settings > Leaves > Leave Policies
Click 'Add New' to create a new leave policy
Choose a template or create from scratch
Define policy name and description
Select applicable leave types (Annual, Sick, Unpaid, Maternity, etc.)
Configure leave pay rates with up to 5 conditions based on number of leave days taken
Set daily wage calculations for unpaid leaves (fully paid, partially paid, or unpaid)
Configure salary calculation formula (basic salary or basic + allowances)
Set working day type (calendar or working days) for calculations
Assign the policy to relevant employees (individual or all employees)
Review and save the policy configuration
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Leave policy is created with customized pay rate conditions, assigned to employees, and ready to automatically apply pay rates based on leave days taken. System enforces compliance with labor law requirements (e.g., UAE/KSA sick leave rules).
rules_and_constraints: Payroll module must be enabled for leave salary features
Salaries for employees must be configured before policy assignment
Can add up to 5 pay rate conditions in a policy
Leave allowance is considered used when leave request is approved
Employees on probation can be restricted from leave salary based on policy configuration
Policy becomes active from the date of activation
Not all employees in a leave policy are automatically included in leave salary policy - must be manually added
Same employees cannot be added to multiple leave salary policies
Accrual pausing based on total days of specified leave types
Prorated calculation using calendar or working days
Compliance with local labor laws (e.g., KSA's 21-day minimum for unpaid leave)
Automatic calculation of reduced monthly accrual
Configuration cannot be edited once set
Only applies to new leave requests
Does not retroactively apply to previous leave requests
Automatic conversion between day types
Adjusted leave days use original request start date
Leave can be calculated as either Calendar Days or Working Days (per U.A.E. Labor Law: 30 Calendar Days OR 22 Working Days for annual leave)
Can specify paid, partially paid, or unpaid leave types
Can set conditional leave allowances based on probation or tenure thresholds
Can exclude public holidays from leave calculations
Can choose between annually recurring or one-time policies
Can enable conditional pay rate adjustments for compliance (sick leave, maternity leave)
Leave salary and encashment only available for Paid leave types (unpaid leave cannot enable these)
Leave days accrue monthly (total annual days divided by 12) for accrual policies
Option to enable negative balances for accrual policies
Can pause monthly accrual during specific leave types
Salary proration available for leave salary policies (calculates pay for days worked before/after leave)
edge_cases: Policy only works when payroll module is enabled
Payroll deduction occurs only if leave request meets specified conditions
Default pay rate applies if no specific condition is met
Leave policy can be edited after creation, but some configurations cannot be changed once employees are assigned
Employees on probation may be restricted from leave salary eligibility
Past leave requests are not affected by new policy activation
Configuration cannot be edited once set - requires creating new policy if changes needed
Only applies to new leave requests, not retroactively
Automatic conversion between day types may occur
Adjusted leave days use original request start date for calculations
Overlapping leave requests can be tracked; affected leave requests will be moved to Pending state when policy is modified
Users can update used leave days manually after policy creation
Policies can be edited or deleted after creation
Enabling calendar/working day calculation will recalculate existing leave requests and move affected requests to Pending state
Calculation depends on company's specific work week and holiday configuration
Employee tenure threshold crossed mid-year affects accrued balance (system auto-adjusts allowance when employee crosses tenure threshold)
Employee transferred between UAE and KSA entities with different labor laws requires policy reassignment
Backdated hire date changes affect tenure calculations and may require leave balance adjustments
Multiple leave policies for same leave type may cause confusion; consolidate where possible
Policy changes affect existing accrued leave; communicate changes to affected employees

steps: Navigate to Finance > Leave Salary
Review pending leave salary requests
Click on a request to view details
Check employee eligibility under the leave salary policy
Review the calculated leave salary amount
Optionally overwrite the amount if needed
Add remarks explaining any changes
Choose action: Approve, Reject, or Add to Payroll Table
If approved, confirm the amount will be added to payroll
Process the leave salary transaction
Verify transaction appears in payroll table
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Leave salary requests are reviewed and processed. Approved amounts are added to the payroll table for the relevant pay period. Finance Manager receives notification of processed transactions.
rules_and_constraints: Leave salary requests are only created for employees eligible under a specific leave salary policy
Once an amount is overwritten and approved, it cannot be changed unless moved back to pending state
Requests can be moved back to pending state if needed for recalculation
Leave salary can be processed in current or different month
Salaries can be prorated based on leave period
Different working day settings can affect calculation
Some allowances might not be included in leave salary
Leave salary and encashment only available for Paid leave types
Unpaid leave cannot have leave salary/encashment enabled
Leave salary and encashment toggles are independent
Multiple paid vacation types can be combined into one leave salary setting
Probation restriction can block leave salary for employees on probation
Salary proration calculates pay for days worked in the month (before/after leave period)
Leave salary policy can only be configured by specific user roles
Leave periods spanning multiple months require split deductions per month
Deductions are non-editable to maintain data consistency
edge_cases: Overwritten amounts cannot be changed after approval
Requests can be moved back to pending state if needed
Leave periods spanning multiple months require special calculation
Advance salary payments need precise tracking and adjustment
Different working day settings can impact salary calculation
Not all employees in a leave policy are automatically included in leave salary policy
Employees must be manually added to leave salary policy
Unpaid leave types cannot enable leave salary/encashment - system enforces this with tooltip
Partial month worked affecting accrual
Employee with multiple leave types
Mid-cycle leave salary requests

what_to_do.task: Request and Apply for Half-Day Leave
steps: Open Bayzat platform (web or mobile app)
Navigate to Time tab (web) or select 'Request a Leave' widget (mobile app)
Click 'Request a Leave' option
Select the leave type from dropdown
Choose the date for half-day leave
Set 'From' date and 'To' date to the same date
Enable the '0.5 days' toggle to indicate half-day
Add any optional comments or notes
Review the leave request details
Click 'Save' and 'Apply' to submit the leave request
Receive confirmation of submission
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Half-day leave request is submitted successfully. Request is routed to appropriate approvers based on company approval flow. Employee receives notification when request is approved or rejected.
rules_and_constraints: Half-day leave can only be applied by using the same date in 'From' and 'To' fields and adjusting to 0.5 days
Leave type must be enabled in leave policies
Employee must have sufficient leave balance for the requested leave type
edge_cases: Half-day leave can only be applied by using the same date in 'From' and 'To' fields and adjusting to 0.5 days

source_articles.title: How to apply for a half day leave

what_to_do.task: View and Manage Overlapping Leave Requests
steps: Navigate to Leaves module
Click on 'Leave Requests' section
Click 'Bayzat Insights' button to view analytics
Review leave balance for all leave types
Check leave history to identify patterns
Identify overlapping leave requests in the insights view
Optional: Open Leaves Calendar for visual representation
View color-coded leave requests (yellow = Pending, green = Accepted)
Click on highlighted overlapping requests to view details
Review overlapping request details and dates
Download leave report for record keeping
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Employee or Manager can identify overlapping leave requests and potential scheduling conflicts. Insights provide visibility into leave balances and history to support better leave planning decisions.
rules_and_constraints: Leave requests are color-coded: yellow for Pending stage, green for Accepted stage
Bayzat Insights button provides leave balance and history information
Leaves Calendar shows visual representation of all leave requests
edge_cases: Users can identify and manage leave requests with potential scheduling conflicts

source_articles.title: How to view overlapping leave requests
How to download leave reports

what_to_do.task: View leave requests on calendar as HR Admin
steps: Navigate to Time Module
Click on Calendar view
Observe color-coded leave requests (green=accepted, yellow=pending)
Click on individual leave requests to view details
Review request details including dates, leave type, and status
Optionally approve or reject pending requests from the calendar view
what_to_do.expected_outcome: HR Admin has full visibility of all team leave requests on a calendar interface, can identify overlapping leaves, and can action pending requests directly from the calendar.
rules_and_constraints: Leave requests are color-coded: green for accepted, yellow for pending
Admin can view and action all leave requests regardless of status
Calendar displays all leave types and statuses in a single view
edge_cases: Overlapping leave requests from multiple team members are visible simultaneously on the calendar

source_articles.title: How to view leave requests on the calendar - Admin

what_to_do.task: Configure and view team leave calendar as Employee
steps: Navigate to Settings > Leaves > Leave Calendar (admin only)
Toggle employee view ON to enable employee access to leave calendar
Customize view preferences for how employees see requested and approved leaves (optional)
As employee: Click 'Time' module
Select 'Calendar' option
Scan leave requests to identify who is on leave, leave type, duration, and status
Click on specific leave request for detailed view (if needed)
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Employees can view colleagues' leave requests on a calendar to plan their own leaves effectively and avoid scheduling conflicts. HR Admin can control whether this visibility is enabled organization-wide.
rules_and_constraints: Organization must explicitly enable employee leave calendar visibility via Settings toggle
Employees will NOT see Messages, Attachments, or Comments associated with leave requests
Toggle can be enabled and disabled at any time
Team visibility can be configured by sharing level: Same Reporting Manager, Department, Office, or All Employees (from PRD)
edge_cases: Employees cannot see sensitive fields (attachments, messages) on colleagues' leave requests
Feature disabled by default - requires admin configuration to activate

source_articles.title: How to view leave requests on the calendar - Employee
How to allow your employees to see the leave calendar

what_to_do.task: Edit leave request at different approval stages as Employee
steps: Check the current status of your leave request (pending or accepted)
If status is PENDING: Use the Edit option on desktop or mobile app to modify dates, leave type, or other details
If status is ACCEPTED: Add a comment to the request and tag the admin/HR
In comment, explain the requested change (date modification, cancellation, etc.)
Submit the comment with admin tag
Wait for admin to review and action the change request
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Employee can modify pending leave requests directly and request changes to accepted leaves through admin intervention, maintaining audit trail via comments.
rules_and_constraints: Pending leave requests can be directly edited by the employee
Accepted leave requests cannot be directly edited - require admin intervention
Changes to accepted requests must be communicated via comment with admin tag
Both desktop and mobile platforms support editing pending requests
edge_cases: Accepted leave requests require admin approval for any modifications
Comment-based change requests create audit trail for compliance

source_articles.title: How to edit leave requests as an Employee

what_to_do.task: Edit leave requests as HR Admin
steps: Navigate to Time module
Select Employee Leaves section
Locate the specific leave request to edit
Click the settings icon on the leave request
Modify leave request fields as needed (dates, leave type, status, etc.)
Review changes for accuracy
Click Submit to save changes
what_to_do.expected_outcome: HR Admin can modify leave requests in any status (pending, accepted, or rejected) to correct errors, update information, or manage exceptions.
rules_and_constraints: Admins can edit leave requests in all statuses - pending, accepted, or rejected
Changes are applied immediately upon submission
Admin edits override employee-submitted data
edge_cases: Admin can edit accepted or rejected requests without requiring re-approval

source_articles.title: How to edit leave requests

what_to_do.task: Set up and manage leave allowances for individual employees
steps: Navigate to employee's profile
Click on the Leaves tab
Review available leave policies for the employee
Select leave policies to activate for this employee
Confirm the number of days allocated for each policy
Enter used days if applicable
Save the configuration
Employee can now view and request these leave types in the Bayzat app
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Individual employee has access to configured leave policies with correct allowances and can submit leave requests through the platform.
rules_and_constraints: Leave policies must be individually activated for each employee
Not all company policies are automatically applied to employees
Employees will only see leave policies that have been specifically activated for them
Allocated and used days must be confirmed during setup
edge_cases: Leave policies are not automatically inherited - each employee requires manual setup
Employees cannot request leave types that have not been activated for them

source_articles.title: How to set up leave allowances for individual employees

what_to_do.task: Configure leave encashment and leave salary policies
what_to_do.persona: HR Admin | Super Admin | Finance Manager
steps: Navigate to Settings > Payroll > Leave Salary Policy (for leave salary) or End of Service Eligibility (for encashment)
Create new leave salary or encashment policy with name and description
Select eligible leave types from dropdown (paid leave types only)
Choose salary components for calculation: Basic Only or Basic + Allowances
Select calculation method for days: Calendar Days, Working Days, or Custom Days
For leave salary: Enable automatic leave salary toggle if desired
Configure salary proration toggle for days worked in the month
Set policy restrictions (e.g., restrict during probation period)
For end-of-service: Configure individual leave types for encashment eligibility
Save policy configuration
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Leave encashment and leave salary policies are configured to automatically calculate and process advance salary payments for leave or end-of-service payouts based on company rules and labor law requirements.
rules_and_constraints: Leave salary and encashment only available for Paid leave types
Unpaid leave cannot have leave salary/encashment enabled (system enforces with tooltip)
Can choose between Basic Only or Basic + Allowances for salary components
Can select different calculation methods: Calendar Days, Working Days, or Custom Days
Leave salary and encashment toggles are independent - can enable one without the other
Multiple paid vacation types can be combined into one leave salary setting
Probation restriction can block leave salary for employees on probation
Salary proration calculates pay for days worked before/after leave period
edge_cases: Super admins can see which eligible paid leave-type policies will be overwritten
Unpaid leave types cannot enable leave salary/encashment - system prevents this
Multiple leave types with different day allowances can be combined into one leave salary setting
Probation employees may be restricted from leave salary based on policy configuration

source_articles.title: Leave encashment for End-of-service

what_to_do.task: Review and approve or reject employee leave requests
steps: Receive notification of pending leave request via Bayzat App or email
Access Employee Leaves tab or navigate to leave requests section
Filter requests by various criteria (employee, leave type, date range, status) if needed
Select a pending leave request to review
Review leave request details: employee name, leave type, dates, duration, and leave balance
Check Bayzat Insights or employee profile for additional context if needed
Verify that the employee has sufficient leave balance
Decide on the request: Approve or Reject
Optionally add comments to the request (e.g., approval conditions, feedback)
Confirm the action (Approve or Reject)
If multi-level approval required: request is routed to next approver in the approval flow
Once all approvers sign off, leave days are automatically deducted from employee balance
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Leave request is approved or rejected. If approved and all required approvers have signed off, leave days are automatically deducted from the employee's leave balance. Employee receives notification of the decision. Request status is updated in the system.
rules_and_constraints: Leave requests must be approved by all managers in the reporting line
For multiple Line Managers, approval requires all Line Managers and either a Super Admin or Time Off Manager
Leave days are automatically deducted only after full approval (all approvers in the chain have approved)
Can set up additional approval levels if the first manager is not the final decision-maker
If any approver in a step rejects, the entire request is rejected
Approval flow can be configured with multiple levels and roles (standard or advanced)
Ultimate approver can skip remaining approval steps for urgent situations
Leave requests can only be edited before approval
Requests spanning two leave cycles require separate submissions
Requests must go through configured approval hierarchy before final approval
Employee can tag Line Manager or HR for assistance
edge_cases: Multi-level approval process requires sign-off from multiple managers, which could potentially delay leave request processing
Approver unavailability (vacation, illness) may delay approval; mitigated by multiple approvers per step or delegation
Overlapping leave requests from team members may require coordination
Leave request spanning multiple months may affect payroll in multiple periods
Retroactive leave requests (for past dates) may require special handling
Leave request submitted after leave has already started may need expedited approval
Employee cancels leave after approval but before leave starts; manager may need to reverse deduction
Leave requests spanning multiple leave cycles cannot be submitted as a single request
Cannot edit leave request after initial approval - must tag admin for changes

source_articles.title: How to manage leave requests
How to request leave as an employee

what_to_do.task: Configure leave approval workflow with customizable steps and approvers
steps: Navigate to Settings > Approval Flows > Leaves
Determine whether to use Standard (company-wide) or Advanced (per policy/department/office) approval flow
For Standard Flow: Define approval steps for the company-wide flow
For Advanced Flow: Create custom approval roles (e.g., Sales Director, CEO, Department Manager, Office Manager) if needed
Select which roles or managers serve as approvers for each step
Configure whether approval is sequential (enforce order) or parallel (any approver can approve)
Add multiple approvers per step for redundancy and to handle unavailability
Designate an 'Ultimate Leave Approver' role that can bypass other approvers for urgent situations
For Advanced Flow: Configure different approval flows for different leave policies, departments, or offices
Assign specific employees or custom roles as approvers in the workflow
Set approval sequence (critical approvers first)
Review the approval flow configuration
Save approval flow settings
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Leave approval workflow is configured with defined steps, roles, and approvers. When employees submit leave requests, the system automatically routes requests to the configured approvers in the correct sequence. Approvers receive notifications and can approve or reject requests. Approval flow is enforced consistently across the organization (or per policy/department/office for advanced flow).
rules_and_constraints: Only Super Admin can configure approval flows
Line Managers must be assigned permissions initially before they can approve leave requests
If any approver in a step rejects, the entire request is rejected
Approval can be configured with multiple levels and roles
Standard flow applies single approval configuration company-wide
Advanced flow allows different workflows per leave policy, department, or office
Sequential approval enforces critical approver first before others
Parallel approval allows any approver in a step to approve
Ultimate approver can approve and skip remaining steps
Custom roles (Sales Director, CEO, Department Manager, Office Manager) work similar to Line Manager (auto-assigned based on structure)
Leave requests can only be edited before approval
Requests spanning two leave cycles require separate submissions
Requests must go through configured approval hierarchy before final approval
Employee can tag Line Manager or HR for assistance
edge_cases: Approver unavailability (vacation, illness, extended leave) requires workaround/delegation feature to prevent bottlenecks
Urgent approval requests when primary approvers unavailable; ultimate approver can expedite
Approver changes mid-request (e.g., manager leaves company); system must handle gracefully
Employee transferred between departments/offices; approval flow may change
Multiple leave types with different approval requirements; advanced flow handles this
Circular approval chains (A approves B, B approves A); system should prevent
Approver has no direct reports; cannot be assigned as Line Manager approver
Leave requests spanning multiple leave cycles cannot be submitted as a single request
Cannot edit leave request after initial approval - must tag admin for changes

source_articles.title: How to set up Leave approval flow
How to assign Line managers for Leaves
How to request leave as an employee

what_to_do.task: Configure leave deductions, encashment, and unpaid leave payroll integration
steps: Navigate to Settings > Leaves > Leave Policies and select or create a leave policy
For unpaid leave setup: Select 'Unpaid' as the leave pay rate option
Configure leave pay rates: choose between Paid, Partially Paid, or Unpaid
If Partially Paid: set the deduction percentage (e.g., 50% salary deduction)
Assign the policy to specific employees or all employees
For leave encashment: Navigate to Settings > Payroll tab
Scroll to 'End of Service' section and click Configure
Select specific leave types eligible for encashment
Choose salary calculation basis: Basic Salary or Basic Salary + Allowances
For public holiday deductions: Navigate to Settings > Company > Holiday Calendars
Select the Holiday Calendar and edit specific public holidays if needed
Change date range for public holidays as needed
Optionally send email notification to affected employees about holiday changes
For unpaid leave payroll integration: Ensure Payroll module is enabled
Verify employee salaries are configured
Upon leave approval, system automatically creates payroll deduction
Review deductions in Payroll > Adjustments page
Process deductions as part of monthly payroll run
Save all configurations
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Leave deductions are configured and automatically applied to payroll. Unpaid leave requests trigger automatic salary deductions based on configured pay rates. Leave encashment is calculated based on selected salary basis. Public holidays are automatically excluded from leave calculations. Payroll processor receives notifications of pending deductions. Deductions are processed with the monthly payroll run.
rules_and_constraints: Payroll module must be enabled for unpaid leave deductions and leave encashment
Salaries must be configured for employees before deductions can be calculated
Leave pay rates can be set as: Paid (no deduction), Partially Paid (partial salary deduction), Unpaid (full salary deduction)
Daily wage calculation based on basic salary divided by 30 days (or working days based on policy)
Leave types must be pre-configured in the system for encashment
Calculation can be based on Basic Salary or Basic Salary + Allowances
Daily rate calculation uses Calendar or Working days based on Time Off policy configuration
Recalculation only works for current or future leave cycles (not previous cycles)
Restriction for editing must be turned ON for the time off policy to prevent manual adjustments
Changes to public holidays affect existing leave requests falling on modified holiday dates
Deduction cannot be edited once created - must adjust the leave request to recalculate
Cannot create deductions for closed payroll months
Multi-month leave creates separate deductions per calendar month
Users can manually adjust leave allowances by enabling a toggle
Pro-rated days include days already used in the current leave cycle
Leave must be fully approved by all approvers in hierarchy before deduction is created
Deductions depend on when leave request is created (current, future, or multiple months)
Closed payroll months require manual intervention by Payroll Manager
Leave request requires approval through configured approval hierarchy
Once fully approved, automatic payroll deduction is created immediately
edge_cases: Leave spanning multiple payroll months creates separate deductions per month
Future month deductions are scheduled but email notification delayed until month opens
Closed payroll months cannot receive new deductions - handled via email notification
Overlapping deductions show remarks similar to recurring deductions
Employee with multiple leave types; system must calculate deductions for each type
Partial day unpaid leave; calculation method must be defined
Leave request cancelled after deduction created; deduction must be reversed
Employee salary changed mid-month; deduction calculation may be affected
Public holiday added/removed mid-leave period; affected leave requests recalculated
Employee on probation with unpaid leave; probation restriction may apply
Pro-rated days include days already used in the current leave cycle
Leave requests for closed payroll months require manual email notification to Payroll Manager

what_to_do.task: Restrict leave requests during employee probation period
steps: Navigate to Settings > Leaves tab
Expand Leave Policies section
Select the specific leave policy to edit (or create a new policy)
Locate the 'Restrict during probation' toggle option
Toggle the option ON to enable probation restriction for this leave type
Verify that probation dates are explicitly entered in employee work profiles
Save the policy configuration
Communicate the restriction to affected employees (those on probation)
what_to_do.expected_outcome: Leave policy is configured to restrict leave requests during probation. Employees with a probation date filled in their work profile cannot request this leave type during their probation period. The system enforces the restriction automatically when employees attempt to submit leave requests.
rules_and_constraints: Employees under probation (with probation date filled in their work profile) cannot request leave for the specified leave type
Restriction only applies if probation date is explicitly entered in the employee's work profile
Probation restriction can block leave salary for employees on probation (if leave salary is enabled)
Restriction applies to the specific leave policy only; other leave types may not be restricted
edge_cases: Employee's probation date not filled in work profile; restriction does not apply
Employee's probation period ends mid-month; system must recognize the change and allow leave requests from that date forward
Employee transferred to different department with different probation rules; probation date may need to be updated
Backdated probation date changes; may affect historical leave requests
Multiple leave policies with different probation restrictions; employees must understand which leaves are restricted

source_articles.title: How to restrict leave requests on probation

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Cross-Cycle Leave Requests Lack Validation & Error Handling
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: When leave requests span multiple leave cycles, the system fails to provide error messages or guidance to users. Additionally, database constraints prevent legitimate updates to leave cycle settings, blocking administrators from correcting or adjusting cycle configurations for employees.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Missing Automated Notifications for Leave Balance & Policy Changes
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The system lacks automated email notifications to alert employees and HR about impending leave balance expirations due to carry-over limits. When public holidays are updated, affected employees and managers are not notified of impacts on existing leave requests. Automatic leave allowance changes triggered by policy rules are not communicated to HR managers or affected employees.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Manager Approval Workflow Lacks Filtering & Navigation
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The admin calendar interface does not clearly separate viewing leave requests from taking approval actions, creating confusion in the approval workflow. Managers cannot filter leave requests to show only their direct reports, forcing them to review all team leave requests. Push notifications for leave request approvals do not navigate users to the relevant leave request screen.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Bulk Operations Unavailable for Leave Management
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The system does not support bulk updates to employee leave balances, requiring time-consuming individual adjustments for manual corrections. Managers cannot bulk update leave request statuses, forcing one-at-a-time processing. Leave type configurations cannot be modified in edit dialogs or through update endpoints, restricting post-setup adjustments.

Tooltips in the leave balance card persist inappropriately during user interactions like scrolling or clicking, creating visual clutter. The attendance table does not automatically refresh after creating a leave request, requiring users to manually reload the page to see updated information.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Historical & Past Date Leave Handling Restrictions
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: The Leave Management system restricts users from working with historical dates and past leave cycles. Employees cannot submit retrospective leave requests within their active cycle, leave reports do not display balance information for periods before 2024, and the system cannot handle leave requests for non-existent or older leave cycles. This creates a rigid system that prevents proper historical leave tracking and retroactive leave management.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Leave Type Classification Cannot Be Modified
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: Once a leave type is assigned (Paid or Unpaid), users cannot change the classification. This prevents flexible leave management and forces HR Admins to delete and recreate leave policies if the leave type needs to be adjusted, reducing operational efficiency.

what_to_watch_out_for.theme: Partial Day Leave & Work Hour Reconciliation Not Supported
what_to_watch_out_for.limitation: After taking a half-day leave, employees cannot check in or reconcile work hours for the remaining portion of the day. The attendance system does not support flexible partial-day leave management, preventing employees from dynamically managing their work time and creating gaps in attendance tracking.

gaps_identified: Finance Manager has no reported issues.
Super Admin has no reported issues.
persona_coverage.coverage_notes: All four personas have at least one reported issue. Finance Manager coverage is limited to payroll deduction feature gap.

uncovered_nav_entries.purpose: Employee structures enable automatic employee assignment in leave policies. Without a structure, the Manage employee assignment button is disabled with tooltip: Automatic assignment is not available for this policy.

execution_order: 2. EXPLORATION - Navigate to feature, discover all UI elements using exploration_phase instructions
3. IDENTIFY ENTITY - Determine the primary item/entity using primary_entity discovery
4. CRUD TEST - Test create, read, update, delete for the primary entity
5. WORKFLOW CHECK - Verify if feature has workflow triggers/actions
6. APPROVAL FLOW - Test approval process if present
7. WHAT_TO_DO - Execute each task from what_to_do array, validate expected outcomes
8. WHAT_TO_WATCH_OUT_FOR - Validate known limitations and edge cases
9. DOCUMENTATION - Capture screenshots of every significant state change
10. REPORT - Generate validation report with pass/fail for each task and issue
